title: Houston Person

infobox.value: (1934-11-10) November 10, 1934 (age 91)  [Florence](/wiki/Florence,_South_Carolina "Florence, South Carolina"), South Carolina, U.S.

[Prestige](/wiki/Prestige_Records "Prestige Records"), [Westbound](/wiki/Westbound_Records "Westbound Records"), [Mercury](/wiki/Mercury_Records "Mercury Records"), [Savoy](/wiki/Savoy_Records "Savoy Records"), [Muse](/wiki/Muse_Records "Muse Records"), [HighNote](/wiki/HighNote_Records "HighNote Records"), [Telarc](/wiki/Telarc_International_Corporation "Telarc International Corporation")

(born November 10, 1934) is an American [jazz](/wiki/Jazz "Jazz") tenor saxophonist and record producer. Although he has performed in the [hard bop](/wiki/Hard_bop "Hard bop") and [swing](/wiki/Swing_music "Swing music") genres, he is most experienced in and best known for his work in [soul jazz](/wiki/Soul_jazz "Soul jazz"). He received the "Eubie Blake Jazz Award" in 1982.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

American jazz saxophonist (born 1934)

| Houston Person | |
| --- | --- |
| [Person performing at the Cellar in 2006](/wiki/File:Houston_Person.jpg "Person performing at the Cellar in 2006") Person performing at the Cellar in 2006 | |
| Background information | |
| Born | (1934-11-10) November 10, 1934 (age 91)  [Florence](/wiki/Florence,_South_Carolina "Florence, South Carolina"), South Carolina, U.S. |
| Genres | [Jazz](/wiki/Jazz "Jazz"), [soul jazz](/wiki/Soul_jazz "Soul jazz"), [swing](/wiki/Swing_music "Swing music") |
| Occupations | Musician, songwriter, bandleader, producer |
| Instrument | Saxophone |
| Labels | [Prestige](/wiki/Prestige_Records "Prestige Records"), [Westbound](/wiki/Westbound_Records "Westbound Records"), [Mercury](/wiki/Mercury_Records "Mercury Records"), [Savoy](/wiki/Savoy_Records "Savoy Records"), [Muse](/wiki/Muse_Records "Muse Records"), [HighNote](/wiki/HighNote_Records "HighNote Records"), [Telarc](/wiki/Telarc_International_Corporation "Telarc International Corporation") |

Musical artist

**Houston Person** (born November 10, 1934) is an American [jazz](/wiki/Jazz "Jazz") tenor saxophonist and record producer. Although he has performed in the [hard bop](/wiki/Hard_bop "Hard bop") and [swing](/wiki/Swing_music "Swing music") genres, he is most experienced in and best known for his work in [soul jazz](/wiki/Soul_jazz "Soul jazz"). He received the "Eubie Blake Jazz Award" in 1982.

## Biography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Houston_Person&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Biography")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/c/c7/Houston_Person_1984.jpg/250px-Houston_Person_1984.jpg)](/wiki/File:Houston_Person_1984.jpg)

Person in New York, 1984

Person grew up in [Florence, South Carolina](/wiki/Florence,_South_Carolina "Florence, South Carolina"), and first played piano, before switching to [tenor saxophone](/wiki/Tenor_saxophone "Tenor saxophone").[[1]](#cite_note-1) He studied at [South Carolina State College](/wiki/South_Carolina_State_College "South Carolina State College"), where he was inducted into the school's Hall of Fame in 1999.

In the [United States Air Force](/wiki/United_States_Air_Force "United States Air Force"), he joined a service band stationed in [West Germany](/wiki/West_Germany "West Germany"), and played with [Don Ellis](/wiki/Don_Ellis "Don Ellis"), [Eddie Harris](/wiki/Eddie_Harris "Eddie Harris"), [Cedar Walton](/wiki/Cedar_Walton "Cedar Walton"), and [Leo Wright](/wiki/Leo_Wright "Leo Wright"). Person later continued his studies at [Hartt College of Music](/wiki/Hartt_College_of_Music "Hartt College of Music") in [Hartford, Connecticut](/wiki/Hartford,_Connecticut "Hartford, Connecticut").

He first became known for a series of albums for [Prestige](/wiki/Prestige_Records "Prestige Records") in the 1960s. Contrary to popular belief, he was never married to the vocalist [Etta Jones](/wiki/Etta_Jones "Etta Jones"), but did spend many years as her musical partner, recording, performing and touring, and for much of his career this association was what he was best known for. They first met playing in organist [Johnny Hammond](/wiki/Johnny_%22Hammond%22_Smith "Johnny \"Hammond\" Smith")'s band.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

There are more than 75 albums recorded by Person as a bandleader, on Prestige, [Westbound](/wiki/Westbound_Records "Westbound Records"), [Mercury](/wiki/Mercury_Records "Mercury Records"), [Savoy](/wiki/Savoy_Records "Savoy Records"), and [Muse](/wiki/Muse_Records "Muse Records"), and he has most recently been recording on [HighNote](/wiki/HighNote_Records "HighNote Records"). He has recorded with [Charles Brown](/wiki/Charles_Brown_(musician) "Charles Brown (musician)"), [Ron Carter](/wiki/Ron_Carter "Ron Carter"), [Bill Charlap](/wiki/Bill_Charlap "Bill Charlap"), [Charles Earland](/wiki/Charles_Earland "Charles Earland"), [Lena Horne](/wiki/Lena_Horne "Lena Horne"), [Etta Jones](/wiki/Etta_Jones "Etta Jones"), [Lou Rawls](/wiki/Lou_Rawls "Lou Rawls"), [Janis Siegel](/wiki/Janis_Siegel "Janis Siegel"), [Horace Silver](/wiki/Horace_Silver "Horace Silver"), [Dakota Staton](/wiki/Dakota_Staton "Dakota Staton"), [Cedar Walton](/wiki/Cedar_Walton "Cedar Walton"), plus [Billy Butler](/wiki/Billy_Butler_(guitarist) "Billy Butler (guitarist)"), [Don Patterson](/wiki/Don_Patterson_(organist) "Don Patterson (organist)"), [Grant Green](/wiki/Grant_Green "Grant Green"), [Sonny Phillips](/wiki/Sonny_Phillips "Sonny Phillips"), [Johnny "Hammond" Smith](/wiki/Johnny_%22Hammond%22_Smith "Johnny \"Hammond\" Smith"), [Richard "Groove" Holmes](/wiki/Richard_%22Groove%22_Holmes "Richard \"Groove\" Holmes")[[3]](#cite_note-allmusic-3) and others.

Person has been a resident of [Newark, New Jersey](/wiki/Newark,_New_Jersey "Newark, New Jersey").[[4]](#cite_note-4)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/4/47/Houston_Person_2009.jpg/250px-Houston_Person_2009.jpg)](/wiki/File:Houston_Person_2009.jpg)

Person performing with his combo at the [Red Bank Jazz & Blues Festival](/wiki/Red_Bank_Jazz_%26_Blues_Festival "Red Bank Jazz & Blues Festival") in 2009.

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/0/0e/Houston_Person_1980.jpg/250px-Houston_Person_1980.jpg)](/wiki/File:Houston_Person_1980.jpg)

Person in [Paris](/wiki/Paris "Paris"), France, in 1980

## Awards

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Houston_Person&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Awards")]

Person received the Eubie Blake Jazz Award in 1982, and in 2011 the Jazz Legend Award, presented in [San Diego](/wiki/San_Diego "San Diego").[[5]](#cite_note-5)

## Discography

### As sideman

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Houston_Person&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: As sideman")]

**With [Joe Alterman](/w/index.php?title=Joe_Alterman&action=edit&redlink=1 "Joe Alterman (page does not exist)")**
